name: Encode export and storage architecture
summary: Two dominant architectures: (A) stream sparse frames through AVFoundation hardware encoder into timelapse-speed MP4, (B) write screenshot sequence then FFmpeg stitch. TimeLapze uses AVFoundation + CoreMedia with hardware acceleration. Segmented recording (chunk files) reduces data loss on crash — pattern from ScreenSage. Export must produce Quick Look-compatible H.264/AAC MP4; Hustl reviews suggest some exports fail standard preview.
key_findings: Direct timelapse encode avoids 7GB/hour full capture problem; FFmpeg skills ecosystem large for post-stitch; Incremental encode during capture lowers peak disk use vs thousands of PNGs; GIF export optional for social but secondary
implications_for_trylapse: Default: AVFoundation VideoToolbox H.264; Background export queue with progress in menu bar; Segment every N minutes to temp dir; Validate export with AVAssetReader smoke test before showing Done; Optional FFmpeg CLI for power users
strengths: Hardware encode on Apple Silicon; Predictable MP4 output
weaknesses: HDR/wide-gamut color accuracy still differentiator hard problem; Long sessions need disk budget planning
